meta.title: ante-

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; the teaching layer (mentor_note, discovery) is authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: Held out ante meridiem deliberately: decoding it reveals what AM has meant the whole time, the exact 'I can understand this now' moment the brief asks for. Owner review pending.

unit.label: ante-
unit.mentor_note: This prefix is easy to confuse with one that looks almost identical and means almost the opposite. Meet it carefully now, and anti- (next) will be easier, not harder.

members.meaning: of care given before birth, during pregnancy

discovery.understand_note: Ante- means before. An antecedent comes before. Antenatal care happens before birth. Antediluvian stretches the idea to its widest reach: before the flood, before recorded memory.

applies.prompt: Knowing ante- means before, what do you think ante meridiem marks?

options.text: A period long before recorded history
